claim: The soviet-communism hybrid-transitional coding — distinct from soviet-union-collapse — risks understating the Stalinist period's clear sacrificial-escalation pattern: collectivization famines, purges, and gulag mobilization represent unbounded sacrifice demands that fit high-pathology coding more precisely than hybrid-transitional.
rationale: The hybrid-transitional code may reflect the full 1917-1985 arc rather than the Stalinist peak; if the case covers the Stalinist period specifically, sacrificial-escalation is a stronger fit. Clarifying the periodization scope is necessary before the outcome code can be treated as theoretically settled.
